questions.question: The weather was cold, _____ we stayed indoors.
questions.explanation: Result: 'so' – we stayed indoors because of the cold.

questions.question: She has lived here _____ 2020.
questions.explanation: Since + a point in time (2020). We use present perfect.

questions.question: I don't know _____ he left.
_____ you like coffee or tea?
questions.explanation: Present simple question: Do you like...?

questions.question: We went out _____ the rain.
questions.explanation: Despite + the rain. Although needs a clause: although it was raining.

questions.question: This is the book _____ I was telling you about.
He _____ have finished by now.
questions.explanation: We think it is true: must have + past participle.

questions.question: There isn't _____ milk left.
questions.explanation: In negative sentences we use any (with uncountable milk).

questions.question: She made the cake _____ hand.
questions.explanation: By hand = she made it herself (fixed phrase).

questions.question: I'll call you _____ I arrive.
The meeting is _____ Monday.
questions.explanation: Days of the week → on (on Monday, on Friday).

questions.question: She lives _____ London.
questions.explanation: Cities and countries → in (in London, in England).

questions.question: The woman _____ lives next door is a doctor.
questions.explanation: Person as subject of the clause → who (or that).

questions.question: This is the house _____ I was born.
I have _____ to London many times.
questions.explanation: Present perfect for life experience: have/has + past participle.

questions.question: Have you _____ been to Japan?
questions.explanation: Life experience (no specific time) → present perfect; 'ever' is common.

questions.question: I want _____ learn English.
I have _____ apple.
_____ sun is shining.
I'll see you _____ 9 o'clock.
questions.explanation: Clock times → at (at 9 o'clock, at noon).

questions.question: We need to do this _____ order to succeed.
questions.explanation: In order to = fixed phrase meaning 'so that we can'.

questions.question: Many people depend _____ their phones every day.
_____ doing so, they saved a lot of time.
questions.explanation: By doing so = by doing that.

questions.question: She is _____ longer interested in the project.
We will continue _____ long as we have support.
questions.explanation: As long as = on condition that.

questions.question: There was _____ much traffic that we were late.
_____ the weather was bad, we enjoyed the trip.
I haven't seen him _____ ages.
questions.explanation: For + period of time with present perfect.

questions.question: This is the best film _____ I have ever seen.
questions.explanation: Superlative + that (or which) in defining relative clause.

questions.question: _____ you mind closing the door?
We have _____ choice but to wait.
questions.explanation: Have no choice but to = must.

questions.question: She arrived _____ the station at 6 p.m.
He is interested _____ learning new languages.
_____ is important to revise regularly.
questions.explanation: It is important to = introductory it.

questions.question: They have lived here _____ 2015.
questions.explanation: Since + point in time with present perfect.

questions.question: I look forward _____ hearing from you.
questions.explanation: Look forward to + -ing (to is a preposition).

questions.question: We succeeded _____ passing the exam.
_____ of the students passed the test.
There were _____ people at the concert.
questions.explanation: Many/a lot of + plural countable noun.

questions.question: She asked _____ I could help her.
He said _____ he was tired.
The keys are _____ the table.
We are proud _____ our team's success.
_____ the meantime, we can wait here.
She is good _____ playing the piano.
I've been waiting _____ over an hour.
He apologized _____ being late.
The book belongs _____ me.
We discussed the problem _____ detail.
_____ general, students prefer shorter exams.
The film was _____ interesting that we watched it twice.
I'll be there _____ a few minutes.
questions.explanation: In + time = after (in a few minutes = soon).
